cheffy_p5_notif_research.objective: Establish grounded facts to build opt-in local cooking-time reminders. Research: (a) the browser flow -- navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js'), Notification.requestPermission(), ServiceWorkerRegistration.showNotification(); (b) the Notification Triggers API (TimestampTrigger / showTrigger) -- current support (Chromium/Android/Pixel) + how to feature-detect it, PLUS the setTimeout-while-page-open fallback for browsers without it and a clean no-op path where unsupported (no Notification / no SW). Target is an Android Pixel + Chrome primarily; do NOT design any iOS-specific path. (c) the cooking-time data source ALREADY on the page: the #cheffy-week-ics JSON island built in Cheffy.astro carries per-week/per-meal ICS + times -- confirm whether to reuse it or add a small parallel times island (prefer reuse; note the exact fields available). Confirm the seam: register 'trigger-permission' via the SAME order-safe window.registerCheffyAction pattern used in src/utils/cheffyCalendarActions.ts, mounted by a SINGLE bundled import line appended to Cheffy.astro; the notifications dialogue node + trigger-permission action id ALREADY exist in cheffy-dialogue.json (do NOT re-author the tree). Deliverable: exact APIs, feature-detection code shape, the degradation matrix, and the times-data path the Planner will blueprint against.

job_cheffy_calendar_build.objective: Implement src/utils/cheffyCalendar.ts per the planner blueprint (auto-attached). Deliver real versions of the three exports, matching the existing stub signatures: extractCookingEvents(markdown, weekStart) -- parse the week Markdown for clock times, durations, semantic times, and any structured time override into CookingEvent[]; buildIcs(events) -- emit a valid RFC 5545 VCALENDAR with one VEVENT per event (UID, DTSTAMP, DTSTART, DTEND from durationMinutes, CRLF line endings, 75-octet line folding, proper text escaping), correct for BOTH a single event (per-recipe mini export) and the whole week (add-all); googleCalendarUrl(event) -- a prefilled calendar.google.com render action=TEMPLATE link. job_cheffy_calendar_research.criterion: calendar-engine: cheffyCalendar.ts time extraction from week Markdown + valid ICS (add-all + per-recipe mini) + Google Calendar template URL; UI-independent.
job_cheffy_calendar_research.objective: Ground the calendar engine for Cheffy (src/utils/cheffyCalendar.ts). Establish three things. (a) RFC 5545 requirements for a valid downloadable .ics: required VEVENT fields (UID, DTSTAMP, DTSTART, DTEND), date-time formatting (UTC Z vs local with TZID), CRLF line endings, 75-octet line folding, text escaping for comma semicolon backslash and newline, and an optional VALARM for reminders. (b) The Google Calendar render template URL format (action=TEMPLATE plus text, dates as YYYYMMDDThhmmss to YYYYMMDDThhmmss, details, location) for a no-download add-event link. (c) The REAL time and duration vocabulary in this repo week Markdown: sample FOOD-OF-THE-WEEK.md, WEEKEND.md and 2-3 archive/ md files and catalog the actual formats that appear (clock times like 3:00 PM or 5:30, semantic phrases like dinner time, durations like 30 minutes or 1 hour, and whether any structured time annotation exists), plus how weekParser.ts exposes content (does the page hand the util raw markdown or only structured ContentSection text) and how weekStart or the week date is derived (e.g. the archive filename date prefix or the H1 title). Deliver concrete findings: the minimal valid ICS skeleton, the Google URL param recipe, a catalogued list of real time patterns with example lines, and the data-access plan to get markdown plus weekStart into extractCookingEvents. Constraint: do NOT add an ICS library or a test framework; hand-rolled dependency-free is the target (flag any blocking reason if infeasible).
job_cheffy_calendar_research.title: research: calendar engine (ICS + time extraction)

job_cheffy_dialogue_build.criterion: dialogue-panel: CheffyPanel.astro + cheffy-dialogue.json data-driven tree with goto navigation, free-text search node, and action dispatch.
job_cheffy_dialogue_build.objective: Execute the Planner blueprint (auto-attached) for part 3/9 dialogue-panel. Scope + checkable acceptance: (1) CheffyPanel.astro renders the current dialogue node from the JSON and navigates CLIENT-SIDE via goto across the whole nodes map (root -> calendar -> back etc.); options are data-driven, no hard-coded lists. (2) A free-text search node accepts input and navigates to a matching archive week or meal (navigate-to-archive works end to end against existing /archive/[slug] routes). (3) close collapses the panel to idle reusing the part-2 path; each node expression drives the mascot via the part-2 cheffySetState hook. (4) The three deferred actions -- generate-ics, trigger-permission, export-checklist -- are dispatched through the action registry and hit a graceful default (no throw; coming-soon/no-op), with a clearly documented registration seam for parts 4/5/6. (5) Pure traversal/search logic lives in a zero-relative-import util verified by an ephemeral Node script. (6) src/data/cheffy-dialogue.json fleshed into the real authored tree, no dangling goto ids. Boundary: <Cheffy /> stays NOT mounted in Layout.astro (part 9); do NOT implement calendar/notification/checklist action logic (parts 4/5/6); do NOT touch cheffyCalendar.ts, public/sw.js, GroceryList.astro; do NOT add motion that violates prefers-reduced-motion. Part-2 open/close/state behavior must remain intact. Done = npm run build (astro check and astro build) passes, no console errors on any page.
job_cheffy_dialogue_build.title: build: dialogue-panel -- CheffyPanel runtime + dialogue.json tree + action registry

job_cheffy_dialogue_plan.criterion: dialogue-panel: CheffyPanel.astro + cheffy-dialogue.json data-driven tree with goto navigation, free-text search node, and action dispatch.
job_cheffy_dialogue_plan.objective: Blueprint the ONE part -- dialogue-panel (part 3/9) -- on the research note (auto-attached). Design, for the builder to execute: (A) CheffyPanel.astro becomes the full CLIENT-SIDE dialogue runtime -- render the current node (text, expression, options, optional free-text input), client-side goto navigation across the WHOLE nodes map (not just the start node), and drive the mascot per node via the part-2 cheffySetState hook (dialogue/processing + expression). (B) An ACTION-DISPATCH REGISTRY contract -- a data-action to handler map with a graceful default -- the hexagonal boundary parts 4/5/6 plug their handler into (generate-ics, trigger-permission, export-checklist) WITHOUT reworking the panel; specify the exact registration seam (e.g. a documented window/root hook) and the graceful no-op/coming-soon default so the panel never throws before those parts land. (C) Fully implement the two pure-navigation actions this part OWNS: close (reuse the part-2 closePanel path) and navigate-to-archive + the free-text search resolution (client navigation to existing /archive/[slug] routes, using the index approach research picked). (D) Flesh src/data/cheffy-dialogue.json from stub into the real authored tree (data, not code) per schema, exposing all five actions + the search node, no dangling goto ids. (E) Put pure logic (dialogue-tree traversal, search matcher) in a ZERO-RELATIVE-IMPORT util (mirror cheffyState.ts / cheffyCalendar.ts) so the TEST rung verifies it with an ephemeral Node script. Boundary the plan must enforce: do NOT implement the three deferred actions logic, do NOT touch cheffyCalendar.ts/sw.js/GroceryList.astro, do NOT mount Cheffy in Layout (part 9), do NOT add motion that violates prefers-reduced-motion (part 7); must not regress part-2 open/close/state behavior; npm run build stays the durable gate.
job_cheffy_dialogue_plan.title: plan: dialogue-panel -- CheffyPanel runtime + tree + action-dispatch registry

job_cheffy_dialogue_research.criterion: dialogue-panel: CheffyPanel.astro + cheffy-dialogue.json data-driven tree with goto navigation, free-text search node, and action dispatch.
job_cheffy_dialogue_research.objective: Ground part 3/9 (dialogue-panel) before it is blueprinted. Establish, with repo + web evidence: (1) The Astro-idiomatic way to ship the FULL dialogue nodes map (src/data/cheffy-dialogue.json) to the browser for CLIENT-SIDE node switching -- CheffyPanel.astro currently server-renders only the start node; compare importing the JSON inside the client script (Astro/Vite JSON import) vs define:vars vs an inlined script type=application/json data island; pick the PE-safe, no-bloat option (site must still behave with JS off). (2) How to enumerate archive weeks + meals for the free-text search node: how src/pages/archive/[slug].astro routing + slugs are produced, the slug rule already fixed in parts 1-2 (GroceryList weekId = H1 lowercased, spaces to hyphens; see cheffyState.ts deriveWeekId), the meal-name source (H2 headings via the same marked lexing parts 1-2 used), and whether a client-consumable index of {slug,title,meals} must be built at compile time (import.meta.glob over archive/*.md) or derived cheaply. (3) The EXACT part-2 seam to build on WITHOUT regressing it: the cheffySetState hook on #cheffy, the #cheffy-panel element, data-start-node, and the aria-expanded/hidden toggle contract in Cheffy.astro -- cite the current lines so the action-dispatch registry is designed additively. (4) A11y conventions for an in-page expandable dialogue (focus management; role=dialog + aria-live=polite already present). Boundary: this part OWNS CheffyPanel.astro + cheffy-dialogue.json only; it does NOT implement generate-ics/trigger-permission/export-checklist logic (parts 4/5/6) and does NOT mount Cheffy in Layout (part 9).

job_cheffy_mascot_build.criterion: mascot-state-machine: Cheffy.astro corner SVG mascot with expression states + idle/attention/dialogue/processing state machine keyed off the per-week localStorage id (visitedWeeks).
job_cheffy_mascot_build.objective: Implement part 2 (mascot-state-machine) per the auto-attached planner blueprint. Replace the Cheffy.astro stub with: the real corner chef-hat SVG mascot (NAMED eye/mouth/dot parts, brand color tokens); the explicit state machine (exactly one of idle, attention, dialogue, processing on #cheffy[data-state]) with CSS-driven expression swap per the blueprint mapping; and the visitedWeeks localStorage logic -- derive weekId with the SAME slug rule as GroceryList.astro (page H1 lowercased, spaces to hyphens), first sight of a new week enters attention plus a notification dot, opening or dismissing the panel marks the week visited and returns to idle. Wire the toggle button to open/close the panel container (set aria-expanded and toggle hidden on #cheffy-panel) = the dialogue state, and expose a documented hook for later parts to set and clear processing. Honor prefers-reduced-motion (no idle motion, no layout shift). If the blueprint calls for it, add a pure importable src/utils/cheffyState.ts (zero relative imports) exporting deriveWeekId plus the visitedWeeks decision helpers, and have the inline script call it. CONSTRAINTS -- progressive enhancement is the hard rule: do NOT mount Cheffy in Layout.astro (deferred to the final pe-build-gate part); do NOT author the CheffyPanel dialogue tree, navigation, or search (that is part 3); do NOT touch cheffyCalendar, the dialogue JSON content, notifications, or checklist. Contribution BEGINS and ENDS in Cheffy.astro plus the optional cheffyState.ts. ACCEPTANCE: astro check passes; the component renders an SVG mascot with named expression parts and a single data-state among idle/attention/dialogue/processing; the visitedWeeks logic reuses the GroceryList weekId rule; reduced-motion disables idle motion; no console errors; props (enabled, corner) and the panel toggle contract stay intact so nothing else breaks and Layout still behaves like master.

job_cheffy_mascot_plan.criterion: mascot-state-machine: Cheffy.astro corner SVG mascot with expression states + idle/attention/dialogue/processing state machine keyed off the per-week localStorage id (visitedWeeks).

(2) The explicit state machine -- states idle, attention, dialogue, processing; exactly one active at a time on #cheffy[data-state]; the transition table and the precise events that drive each transition IN THIS PART (idle<->attention from visitedWeeks; idle<->dialogue from the toggle button opening/closing the panel container). (4) The toggle/open-close wiring -- aria-expanded on the button, hidden on #cheffy-panel, toggling panel VISIBILITY only. (6) The BUILD boundary -- the builder touches ONLY Cheffy.astro plus the optional cheffyState.ts; must NOT mount Cheffy in Layout.astro, must NOT author CheffyPanel content, must NOT touch cheffyCalendar, dialogue JSON content, notifications, or checklist. job_cheffy_mascot_research.criterion: mascot-state-machine: Cheffy.astro corner SVG mascot with expression states + idle/attention/dialogue/processing state machine keyed off the per-week localStorage id (visitedWeeks).
job_cheffy_mascot_research.objective: Ground part 2 (mascot-state-machine) for Cheffy. Establish concretely: (1) THE canonical week identity -- GroceryList.astro derives its per-week localStorage key from the page H1 as grocery-list-(h1.textContent lowercased, spaces to hyphens). Confirm this is the same week id used elsewhere and catalog ALL per-week localStorage keys/namespaces in the repo, so visitedWeeks reuses the SAME identity (not a parallel one). Pin the exact slug rule and judge whether a small pure importable helper (deriveWeekId) is warranted for testability following the part-1 pattern of a zero-relative-import .ts. (2) The existing Astro client-script + scoped-style convention (see LunchGroceryAddon.astro and GroceryList.astro): how inline script tags hydrate per page, the DOMContentLoaded guard, scoped style tags, and reading the H1 at runtime. (3) The brand chef-hat visual + color tokens: the hat SVG already used (StickyHeader.astro, public/favicon.svg, src/assets/cheffy-animated.svg) and the CSS variables / tailwind tokens (tailwind.config.mjs, src/styles/global.css -- e.g. the primary, bg, and the F3CA40 hat-band color) so the mascot matches brand. (4) SVG expression-swap technique: drive the expressions (neutral, excited, thinking, sleepy) plus a blink and the mouth states (neutral, smile, surprised, frown) plus a notification dot by swapping NAMED parts via CSS keyed on data-state / data-expression (CSS-first so it degrades well). Produce the state-to-expression mapping per the CHEFFY-SYSTEM state table. (5) prefers-reduced-motion: confirm there is no existing handling and establish the pattern to gate idle blink/motion behind a no-preference media query with no layout shift. (6) The progressive-enhancement constraint: Cheffy stays UNMOUNTED in Layout.astro until the final pe-build-gate part, so it cannot be tested via the live site -- recommend a no-framework testability approach for the TEST rung (ephemeral Node import of the pure helper plus astro check plus static structural assertions).
